anchor: The Études d'épistémologie génétique monograph (PUF / Centre, Geneva) crossed with an engraved malacological plate — the conservation-task line diagram, the INRC lattice, and the Limnaea pond-snail shell-spiral as ornament, set in the clean academic type of a mid-century Swiss-French scientific book.
rationale: Piaget began as a naturalist drawing Limnaea molluscs and ended building diagrams of the growing mind at the Geneva Centre; the look fuses the engraved naturalist's plate (sepia, spiral) with the Genevan academic monograph (parchment, ink, a clean reasoning-blue and pond-green) and the structural line-art of his conservation tasks and INRC group. Teal-green carries equilibration/growth and the immanence pole; sepia the biological origin; slate-blue the logico-mathematical structure; ochre-gold 'value' and the sacred; crimson transcendence/constraint. The half-filled circle ornament is the balance of assimilation and accommodation.

fonts.display: Charter, 'Bitstream Charter', Cambria, 'Iowan Old Style', Georgia, serif
fonts.body: Cambria, Charter, Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif
fonts.mono: 'Courier New', 'Cascadia Mono', Consolas, 'DejaVu Sans Mono', monospace
